memberdef.definition: typedef XStatus(* XClock_SetRateFuncPtr)(u8 NodeIdx, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.argsstring: )(u8 NodeIdx, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.name: XClock_SetRateFuncPtr

memberdef.definition: typedef XStatus(* XClock_SetParentFuncPtr)(u8 NodeIdx, u8 SetParentIdx)
memberdef.name: XClock_SetParentFuncPtr

detaileddescription.para: : PSS REF CLK frequency is obtained from xparamters.h and has minor variations based on board revisions. Other frequencies are independent to board revisions and no macros for them are defined in xparameters.h

memberdef.name: DivMap
memberdef.initializer: = { XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(IOPLL_TO_FPD, IOPLL_TO_FPD_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(RPLL_TO_FPD, RPLL_TO_FPD_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(APLL_TO_LPD, APLL_TO_LPD_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(DPLL_TO_LPD, DPLL_TO_LPD_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(VPLL_TO_LPD, VPLL_TO_LPD_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(DBG_TSTMP, DBG_TSTMP_DIV0), XCLOCK_DIV_ASSIGN_INDICES(DDR_REF, DDR_REF_DIV0), }

memberdef.name: FixedFactors
memberdef.initializer: = { XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_PLL, IOPLL_INT_PLL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_PLL, RPLL_INT_PLL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_PLL, APLL_INT_PLL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_PLL, DPLL_INT_PLL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_PLL, VPLL_INT_PLL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_FIXEDFACTORS(XCLOCK_TYPE_DIVIDER, ACPU_DIV0), }

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for clocking device in the system.

detaileddescription.para: configuration table defined in xclockps_g.c

memberdef.name: MuxMap
memberdef.initializer: = { XCLOCK_MUX_ASSIGN_INDICES(GEM_TSU, GEM_TSU), XCLOCK_MUX_ASSIGN_INDICES(CAN0, CAN0), XCLOCK_MUX_ASSIGN_INDICES(CAN1, CAN1), XCLOCK_MUX_ASSIGN_INDICES(DLL_REF, DLL_REF), XCLOCK_MUX_ASSIGN_INDICES(WDT, WDT), }

memberdef.name: Plls
memberdef.initializer: = { XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS(XCLOCK_TYPE_MUX, IOPLL_PRE_SRC_MUX, IOPLL_CTRL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS(XCLOCK_TYPE_MUX, RPLL_PRE_SRC_MUX, RPLL_CTRL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS(XCLOCK_TYPE_MUX, APLL_PRE_SRC_MUX, APLL_CTRL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS(XCLOCK_TYPE_MUX, DPLL_PRE_SRC_MUX, DPLL_CTRL), XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS(XCLOCK_TYPE_MUX, VPLL_PRE_SRC_MUX, VPLL_CTRL), }

detaileddescription.para: configuration table defined in xclockps_g.c

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to read
is the pointer to variable holding read value

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful
Read from register may fail in case driver doesn't have proper access at EL1 NONSECURE

parameterdescription.para: is the address of the register to write
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful XST_FAILURE if unsuccessful
Write to register may fail in case driver doesn't have proper access at EL1 NONSECURE.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XClockPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xclockps.h for the definition of XClockPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 DivIndex, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.name: XClock_DivSetRate

memberdef.argsstring: (u16 *ParentGroup, u8 GroupType, u8 Par1, u8 Par2, u8 Par3)
memberdef.name: XClock_AllocPeriphMuxParents

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 PllIndex, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate *RoundRate)
memberdef.name: XClock_PllRoundRate

memberdef.argsstring: (u8 PllIndex, XClockRate ParentRate, XClockRate Rate, XClockRate *SetRate, u8 DryRun)
memberdef.name: XClock_PllSetRate

briefdescription.para: Clock is critical and will not be disabled.
Gives the max value for unsigned type Note: Not to be used with signed types.

detaileddescription.para: Parent ID is combination of parent type and node indices. Most significant 8 bits represent type of parent and least significant 8 bits represents the node index in nodes database

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_LIMIT_VALUE
XCLOCK_VALIDATE_INDEX

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if index is valid.

detaileddescription.para:
type corresponds to node type and can have either of below:
IP
OP
PLL
MUX
GATE
DIVIDER
FIXEDFACTOR
The macro assumes the index to have unsigned data type and ignores lower bound checks.

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_VALIDATE_INDEX_WARN
memberdef.initializer: do { \ if (MAX_##type < index) { \ xil_printf("Warning: Index %d out of bound for %s\n", \ index, #type); \ return; \ } \ } while (0)

briefdescription.para: This macro checks for valid index and returns no failure status.

detaileddescription.para: The macro assumes the index to have unsigned data type and ignores lower bound checks.

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_VALIDATE_PTR

briefdescription.para: Assign structure elements for dividers with control register shift of 8 bits.
Assign structure elements for dividers with control register shift of 16 bits.

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_GATES
memberdef.initializer: { \ .BitIndex = BitVal, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .Parent = XCLOCK_GENERATE_PARENT_ID(ParentTypeVal, GateIndexVal), \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ }

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_MUXES
memberdef.initializer: { \ .Shift = ShiftVal, \ .Width = WidthVal, \ .ActiveParent = (u8)XCLOCK_INVALID_PARENT, \ .DefaultParent = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .NumParents = NumParVal, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .Parent = ParentVal, \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ }

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_MUXES_P8
memberdef.initializer: { \ .Shift = ShiftVal, \ .Width = WidthVal, \ .ActiveParent = (u8)XCLOCK_INVALID_PARENT, \ .DefaultParent = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .NumParents = 8, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .Parent = ParentVal, \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ }

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_MUXES_P4
memberdef.initializer: { \ .Shift = ShiftVal, \ .Width = WidthVal, \ .ActiveParent = (u8)XCLOCK_INVALID_PARENT, \ .DefaultParent = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .NumParents = 4, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .Parent = ParentVal, \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ }

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_MUXES_P2
memberdef.initializer: { \ .Shift = ShiftVal, \ .Width = WidthVal, \ .ActiveParent = (u8)XCLOCK_INVALID_PARENT, \ .DefaultParent = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .NumParents = 2, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .Parent = ParentVal, \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ }

memberdef.name: XCLOCK_ASSIGN_PLLS
memberdef.initializer: { \ .LockIndex = 0, \ .EnableCount = 0, \ .IsInit = FALSE, \ .Flags = XCLOCK_CLOCK_IS_CRITICAL, \ .Parent = XCLOCK_GENERATE_PARENT_ID(ParentTypeVal, PllIndexVal), \ .CtrlReg = CtrlRegVal, \ .StatusReg = PLL_STATUS, \ .Rate = XCLOCK_INVALID_RATE, \ }

briefdescription.para: Contains the implementation of interface functions of the clock driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains method for static initialization (compile-time) of the driver.
This file handles PLL related definition and operations.
This file handles Mux related definition and operations.
This file contains the hardware details for the System Clock controller.
This file handles Gate related definitions and operations.
This file contains a table that specifies the configuration of the clocking in the system.
This file handles fixed factor related definition and operations.
The Xilinx Clock controller driver provides APIs to control clock parameters.
See xclock.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.00 sd 07/26/18 Fix coverity warnings 1.1 aru 03/20/19 Fix IAR issue by changing "XCLOCK_ABS_DIFF" to a function named "XClock_Absolute_Difference". 1.2 sd 02/13/20 Rename ARRAY_SIZE 1.5 sd 07/10/23 Added SDT support

detaileddescription.para:
Below are the feature of the driver:
Manages database for mux, plls, gates, dividers and fixed factors for all the supported clocks.
Allows to enable/disable specific clock.
Allows to change the parent for specific clock.
Allows to get/set rate for specific clock.

This driver is intended to be RTOS and processor independent. It works with physical addresses only. Any needs for dynamic memory management, threads or thread mutual exclusion, virtual memory, or cache control must be satisfied by the layer above this driver.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.00 sd 07/26/18 Fix Doxygen warnings 1.1 aru 03/20/19 Fix IAR issue by changing "XCLOCK_ABS_DIFF" to a function named "XClock_Absolute_Difference". 1.2 sd 02/13/20 Rename ARRAY_SIZE 1.4 sd 12/02/21 Fix compilation warnings reported with "-Wundef" flag. 1.5 sd 07/10/23 Added SDT support 1.6 sd 11/03/23 Fix the redefinition of the PSS_REF frequency 1.7 vlt 12/30/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 1.7 vlt 04/14/26 Updated LookupConfig BaseAddress type from u32 to UINTPTR to support 64-bit addressing

detaileddescription.para: See xclock.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.2 sd 02/13/20 Rename ARRAY_SIZE 1.7 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.2 sd 02/13/20 Rename ARRAY_SIZE 1.7 vlt 01/27/26 Fixed codespell issues

detaileddescription.para: Each device should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.6 sd 10/30/23 Fix the redefinition of the PSS_REF

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 cjp 02/09/18 First release 1.7 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 1.7 vlt 03/14/26 Updated BaseAddress type from u32 to UINTPTR to support 64-bit addressing.
